summary: Frontend-only landing validation completed on http://localhost:8082 for client and business pages (desktop/mobile). Core navigation and primary CTA interactions were tested, overflow checks passed, but typography requirement (Inter-only visible marketing content) is not met in runtime rendering.

ui_bugs.issue: Runtime font probe returned fallback serif ("Times New Roman") on visible hero marketing text instead of Inter.

integration_issues.flow: Business footer back-to-client navigation on mobile
integration_issues.issue: Could not locate footer link selector during automated mobile run; needs manual confirmation that footer CTA is reachable/clickable on 390x800.

issues: Decorative glow/spotlight effects are still present (hero glow/orb/radial effects), which may conflict with the requested stricter minimal high-end aesthetic if the target is further ornament reduction.

action_items: Fix landing typography runtime so visible marketing text resolves to Inter across both pages (not fallback serif).
Verify mobile footer CTA visibility/clickability for business page at 390x800 and add/adjust stable test target if needed.
If strict minimal direction is required, remove remaining decorative glow/spotlight layers for cleaner visual hierarchy.
critical_code_review_comments: _layout.tsx still loads non-Inter families (Fraunces/Geist) globally while landing spec requires Inter-only visible marketing typography; ensure landing-visible text cannot fall back outside Inter.
Landing motion/effects stack still includes spotlight/tilt/glass decorative layers; this can drift from the requested minimal Superhuman/Mercury direction if not intentionally constrained.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated: client hero CTA scroll/focus to search input works, client->business navigation works, business login CTA routes to /login, sandbox tabs and role selector interactions work, no horizontal overflow at 1920x800 and 390x800 for tested pages. Fresh screenshots were captured under /app/test_reports/artifacts_iteration_19.
rca of the issue: Typography mismatch likely comes from runtime font mapping/fallback: landing components specify Inter token families (Inter_600SemiBold/Inter_700Bold), but computed style probes in browser returned fallback serif for hero text. Repro: open / and /para-estabelecimentos in preview, inspect hero heading computed font-family. Mitigation: confirm expo-font registration to RN web style mapping for Inter token names and enforce explicit web-safe Inter stack for landing-marketing text nodes.
